briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome to the climate briefing for May 9, 2026.

sections.title: ⚠️ Accelerating Ecosystem and Agricultural Threats from Climate Change
sections.content: Climate change is causing significant shifts in Korea's agricultural landscape, with projections indicating that areas suitable for apple cultivation could disappear by the 2090s [2]. Globally, plants are being forced to shift their habitats due to climate change, but many are struggling to find suitable new locations, raising concerns about ecosystem disruption [8]. Furthermore, a serious warning has been issued that Antarctic instability could transform the ocean into a major driver of climate change by 2030 [11]. These reports illustrate the widespread and urgent threats of climate change to global ecosystems and food security.

sections.title: 🔴 Climate Change and Health Threats: Hantavirus Spread
sections.content: The direct threats of climate change to human health are also intensifying. Notably, a surge in hantavirus cases has been observed in Argentina, with experts linking this to climate change [13, 16]. A serious warning has also been issued that the spread of the deadly Andes hantavirus is accelerating due to climate change [18]. This clearly demonstrates that climate change can directly lead to public health crises beyond mere environmental issues, signaling the need for immediate international response.

sections.title: 🌱 Economic Viability of Renewable Energy and Carbon Neutrality Efforts
sections.content: Meanwhile, positive progress in responding to the climate crisis has also been reported. According to an IRENA report, the economic viability of the renewable energy transition has been further strengthened, as solar and wind energy combined with battery storage systems have been officially confirmed to be more cost-effective and reliable than fossil fuels [5]. Domestically, Geoje City has designated Geoje University as its carbon-neutral practice institution for May, encouraging local-level efforts [3]. Internationally, a Polish city ranked first in the EU's climate change resilience index, presenting an exemplary case of climate adaptation [10]. These examples emphasize both the feasibility of climate change response and the importance of concrete actions.

sections.title: 🌍 Conflicting Movements and Challenges in Climate Action
sections.content: However, movements hindering climate change response continue, necessitating vigilance. With Governor DeSantis signing a bill that halts local climate change mitigation efforts, some regions are showing a regression in climate action [9]. Furthermore, major oil companies are demanding immunity from the costs of climate change damages, exacerbating debates over corporate social responsibility [14]. These conflicting movements demonstrate that international solidarity and efforts to overcome the climate crisis still face complex and difficult challenges.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted the threats posed by climate change, the efforts to overcome them, and the challenges that still remain. Through sustained attention and international cooperation, we must wisely respond to the climate crisis. This concludes today's climate briefing.
